title: Spotlight | NYU Shanghai
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU New York City.
text:
Spotlight
A Virtual Celebration of Chinese CuisineOur community shares stories behind their favorite homemade meals.
How will China’s Economy Recover from COVID-19? NYU Shanghai Professors Offer Insights Three professors share their take on China's path to economic recovery as businesses resume in the country
Making Sense of Data: How NYU Shanghai is staying on the cutting edge of the data revolutionNYU Shanghai is working on the cutting edge of the field of data science.
一个人的城市 One Person CityNYU Shanghai alumna Nicole Chan '17 documents Shanghai's jarring emptiness.
Institute of Contemporary Arts Provides New Venue for Artistic CollaborationAfter a two-year hiatus, NYU Shanghai’s art gallery opened October 10 with a new name.
What I'm Reading: Vice Chancellor Jeffrey LehmanVice Chancellor Lehman shares six books that shaped his thinking and understanding of the world in 2019.
Tasting the Past: Food in Chinese History“Some don’t think food is a serious topic. Whereas in fact it applies to everything,” says Waley-Cohen.
A Piece of HomeNYU Shanghai students show us what they brought to remind them of home.
2019 Fall Study Away Photo ContestThe winning entries are here!
Meet Our FreshmenLet’s meet some first years from the class of 2023!
How to Make Better DecisionsAssistant Professor Julia Hur warns against relying on monetary incentives to boost productivity.
OA Advice to First Years10 pieces of sage advice to get you through your first year from your 2019 Orientation Ambassadors.
How Winning Can Actually Lead to LosingProfessor Zhao Bin discusses how psychology influences market trends.
Is It Time for a Sale? Professor Jin Huang Parses the Economics Behind DiscountsProfessor Jin Huang explains how she applies economic theories to price reduction data.
Students Give the Scoop on Summer InternshipsFrom Shanghai to New York to the Netherlands, our students were busy this summer filling internships in everything from fintech, marketing, robotics/AI, environmental conservation, international relations, law and connecting charities with local businesses. The Career Development Center Summer Service Grant supported several students who chose to work for non-for-profit organizations…
From Shanghai to New York to the Netherlands, our students were busy this summer filling internships in everything from fintech, marketing, robotics/AI, environmental conservation, international relations, law and connecting charities with local businesses. The Career Development Center Summer Service Grant supported several students who chose to work for non-for-profit organizations…

The Career Development Center Summer Service Grant supported several students who chose to work for non-for-profit organizations…
